proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively associated with pfs_months: higher values predict longer PFS.
feature_014 has a graded negative association with pfs_months: levels 0 -> 1 -> 2 correspond to progressively shorter PFS.
Patients with feature_006 = 'squamous' have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_006 = 'adenocarcinoma'.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.spearmanr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman correlation between feature_015 and pfs_months is rho=0.769 (p<1e-300); a median split confirms the association: PFS = 4.82 months above the median feature_015 vs 2.04 below (t-test p<1e-300). feature_015 is the single strongest predictor in the cohort.
Mean pfs_months by feature_014: 0 -> 4.33, 1 -> 3.19, 2 -> 2.15 (one-way ANOVA p<1e-300). Strict monotone decrease in PFS with increasing feature_014 level.
Mean pfs_months: adenocarcinoma 3.67 vs squamous 2.81 (t-test p<1e-300). Squamous histology associated with ~0.86 months shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_023 = 0.
feature_011 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_011 = 0.
feature_001 categories differ in pfs_months: never smokers and former smokers have longer PFS than current smokers.

analyses.result_summary: feature_023=0 mean PFS 4.36 vs feature_023=1 mean PFS 2.92 (t-test p<1e-300). feature_023=1 cuts PFS by ~1.44 months.
feature_011=0 mean PFS 3.66 vs feature_011=1 mean PFS 2.74 (p<1e-300). feature_011=1 reduces PFS by ~0.92 months.
Mean PFS: current 2.99, former 3.61, never 3.67 (ANOVA p=3.7e-187). Current smokers have ~0.62-0.69 months shorter PFS than former/never smokers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the continuous laboratory-like features, feature_017 is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_025 is positively associated with pfs_months.
feature_032 is negatively associated with pfs_months.

Most other continuous laboratory features (feature_003, feature_007, feature_008, feature_009, feature_010, feature_013, feature_024, feature_026, feature_029, feature_030, feature_033) have no detectable association with pfs_months in univariate analysis.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = -0.125 (p=1.8e-172). Quartile means: Q1=3.67, Q2=3.35, Q3=3.03 (Q4 capped due to ties). feature_017 has a moderate negative association with PFS.
Spearman rho = +0.100 (p=6.3e-111). feature_025 weakly but very significantly positively associated with PFS.
Spearman rho = -0.014 (p=0.0022). Statistically significant due to large N but effect size negligible; in multivariable model, coef = -0.0004 per unit (p<1e-25).

analyses.code: for c in cont_labs: stats.spearmanr(df[c], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho values (univariate p): feature_003 -0.002 (0.66), feature_007 +0.003 (0.50), feature_008 +0.001 (0.86), feature_009 -0.006 (0.20), feature_010 +0.005 (0.29), feature_013 -0.004 (0.40), feature_024 0.000 (0.99), feature_026 -0.007 (0.13), feature_029 -0.003 (0.51), feature_030 -0.007 (0.13), feature_033 +0.005 (0.29). None reach significance; these features behave as noise covariates with respect to PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features that have a marginal effect on PFS, feature_018 = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_018 = 0.
feature_016 = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_016 = 0 (note: opposite direction from most binary features).
feature_028 = 1 (rare) is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_028 = 0.
feature_031 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_031 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_018=0 mean PFS 3.32 vs feature_018=1 mean 3.64 (t-test p=1.6e-53); feature_018=1 adds ~0.32 months PFS overall.
feature_016=0 mean PFS 3.33 vs feature_016=1 mean 4.11 (p=4.8e-151). feature_016=1 prolongs PFS by ~0.78 months marginally.
feature_028=0 mean PFS 3.47 vs feature_028=1 mean 2.66 (p=2.9e-69). feature_028=1 reduces PFS by ~0.81 months.
feature_031=0 mean PFS 3.52 vs feature_031=1 mean 3.32 (p=2.4e-24). feature_031=1 reduces PFS by ~0.20 months marginally.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Some binary features (feature_012, feature_020, feature_027, feature_002) have no association with pfs_months, indicating they may be randomized or non-prognostic indicators.
feature_021 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_021 = 0 in univariate analysis, but this effect is confounded by smoking status (feature_001).

feature_022 = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_022 = 0 in univariate analysis, but this association is largely explained by smoking status (feature_022 is concentrated in never smokers).

analyses.result_summary: Marginal PFS differences (mean1 - mean0) and t-test p-values: feature_012 -0.035 (p=0.081), feature_020 -0.025 (p=0.255), feature_027 +0.004 (p=0.870), feature_002 +0.009 (p=0.746). All non-significant; these features have ~50%, 30%, 30%, 15% prevalence with no detectable PFS effect.

Univariate: feature_021=1 mean PFS 3.30 vs feature_021=0 mean 3.49 (p=9.1e-18). After adjusting for feature_001 (smoking) and feature_006 (histology), feature_021 coefficient = -0.033 (p=0.144), no longer significant. feature_021 is concentrated in current smokers (45%) vs never smokers (8%), explaining the marginal effect.

Univariate: feature_022=1 mean PFS 3.60 vs feature_022=0 mean 3.41 (p=1.9e-10). feature_022 is heavily concentrated in never smokers (51% prevalence) vs current smokers (3%). After adjusting for smoking and histology, the apparent benefit is nearly eliminated. In the full multivariable model, feature_022 coefficient = -0.001 (p=0.94).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 has the baseline-balance profile of a randomized assignment: its prevalence is independent of feature_015, feature_001, feature_006, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, biomarker indicators, and lab values.
Among binary candidates with non-trivial PFS associations, feature_018 is the most plausible 'treatment' indicator and its effect should be tested for heterogeneity across covariates.

analyses.code: for each control compute chi2/t-test of feature_018 vs control
analyses.result_summary: Sum of balance chi-square statistics across 12 baseline covariates: feature_018 = 5.2 (1 small significant violation), feature_012 = 10.5, feature_020 = 15.3, feature_027 = 6.6, feature_002 = 7.8, feature_031 = 6.3 — all consistent with random assignment. By contrast, feature_021 chi-sum = 4639 (5 large violations) showing strong baseline confounding, ruling it out as a 'random' assignment. feature_018 has the cleanest balance profile combined with a non-zero PFS effect, marking it as the primary treatment-of-interest.

In the full multivariable linear model adjusted for all features, feature_018 coefficient = +0.323 months (SE 0.007, t=47.4, p<1e-300). Effect size is essentially identical to the unadjusted estimate, consistent with random/balanced assignment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_018 on pfs_months differs by feature_006 (histology), with a larger absolute benefit in squamous than in adenocarcinoma.
The treatment effect of feature_018 differs by feature_001 (smoking status), with a larger benefit in current/former smokers than in never smokers.

analyses.code: for hist in df['feature_006'].unique(): test feature_018 effect
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect of feature_018 (mean1 - mean0) by histology: adenocarcinoma +0.277 months (p=7.4e-30), squamous +0.449 months (p=8.0e-32). Both significant; effect modestly larger in squamous (interaction p ~ 0.001 in linear model).

analyses.code: for sm in ('current','former','never'): test feature_018 effect; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018 * C(feature_001)')
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect of feature_018 by smoking: current +0.512, former +0.300, never +0.014. Interaction term p=1.7e-13. Treatment effect monotonically declines from current smokers to never smokers; never smokers have essentially no benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016 is a major effect-modifier of feature_018: among feature_016=1 patients the treatment effect is much larger than among feature_016=0 patients.
feature_031 is a major effect-modifier of feature_018: feature_018 produces a benefit in feature_031=0 patients but not in feature_031=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect (mean1-mean0) by feature_016: feature_016=0 -> -0.005 months (NS); feature_016=1 -> +2.55 months (p<1e-300). Interaction coefficient +2.56 (p effectively 0). Among feature_016=1 patients, feature_018 nearly doubles PFS.

Treatment effect (mean1-mean0) by feature_031: feature_031=0 -> +0.571; feature_031=1 -> +0.009. Interaction coefficient -0.562 (p=5.3e-41). The marginal benefit of feature_018 in the full cohort is driven entirely by feature_031=0 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028 is an effect-modifier of feature_018 that suppresses the treatment effect in feature_028=1 patients.
feature_022 modifies the treatment effect of feature_018 modestly, with smaller benefit in feature_022=1 patients.
feature_005 (rare, ~3% prevalence) suppresses the treatment effect of feature_018 in feature_005=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect by feature_028: feature_028=0 -> +0.345; feature_028=1 -> -0.143. Interaction coefficient -0.488 (p=4.1e-7). Among feature_028=1 patients, feature_018 has no benefit and a non-significant negative point estimate.

Treatment effect by feature_022: feature_022=0 -> +0.353; feature_022=1 -> +0.112. Interaction coefficient -0.241 (p=9.9e-5). Modestly smaller benefit in feature_022=1 patients but effect not abolished.
Treatment effect by feature_005: feature_005=0 -> +0.332; feature_005=1 -> -0.002. Interaction coefficient -0.334 (p=0.006). Treatment benefit is essentially abolished in feature_005=1 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_018 is concentrated in the joint subgroup feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0; in all other strata of (feature_016, feature_031) the effect is null.
Within feature_016=0 patients, no level of any other variable produces a non-null feature_018 treatment effect.

analyses.code: for f16 in [0,1] for f31 in [0,1]: compute feature_018 effect
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect of feature_018 by joint (feature_016, feature_031) subgroup: (0,0) diff=-0.019 NS; (0,1) diff=+0.012 NS; (1,0) diff=+4.644 (p<1e-300); (1,1) diff=-0.012 NS. The +4.6-month benefit appears only when feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0.

analyses.code: for c in covars stratify within feature_016=0
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=0 (n=43,632), feature_018 treatment effect is null in every stratum tested: smoking (current 0.022, former -0.020, never -0.017), histology (adenocarcinoma -0.018, squamous +0.013), feature_031 (0 -> -0.019, 1 -> +0.012), and feature_028 (0 -> 0.000, 1 -> -0.121 NS). No subgroup of feature_016=0 shows a treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_016=1, feature_031=0 responder stratum, feature_028=1 abrogates the feature_018 treatment effect.
Within the feature_016=1, feature_031=0 responder stratum, feature_005=1 abrogates the feature_018 treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1, feature_031=0: feature_028=0 (n=3370) -> treatment diff = +4.832 (p<1e-300); feature_028=1 (n=138) -> treatment diff = -0.285 (p=0.51). feature_028=1 fully abrogates the response.

Within feature_016=1, feature_031=0, feature_028=0: feature_005=0 (n=3266) -> treatment diff = +4.985 (p<1e-300); feature_005=1 (n=104) -> treatment diff = +0.053 (p=0.91). feature_005=1 fully abrogates the response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete responder definition is feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0; this stratum has a feature_018 treatment effect of approximately +5 months while all other patients have a null effect.
The treatment-by-responder interaction effect is robust to multivariable adjustment for prognostic covariates.

analyses.code: df['responder'] = ((df.feature_016==1)&(df.feature_031==0)&(df.feature_028==0)&(df.feature_005==0)).astype(int); compare feature_018 effect by responder
analyses.result_summary: Responder count = 3266 (6.53% of cohort). Within responders: feature_018=0 mean PFS 3.249 (n=2112), feature_018=1 mean 8.234 (n=1154), diff = +4.985 months (p<1e-300). Outside responders: diff = -0.006 months (p=0.76). Subgroup-treatment interaction is essentially perfect.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018*responder + feature_015 + C(feature_001) + C(feature_006) + C(feature_014) + feature_023 + feature_011 + feature_025 + feature_017 + feature_032', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted for major prognostic features: feature_018 main coef = +0.001 (p=0.66), responder main coef = -0.006 (p=0.40), interaction feature_018*responder = +4.927 (p effectively 0). Treatment effect outside the responder subgroup is essentially zero; inside, ~+5 months PFS gain.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup, the feature_018 treatment effect is similar across feature_001 (smoking), feature_006 (histology), feature_014, and feature_023 strata, indicating these are not additional gating variables.

Within the responder subgroup, the feature_018 treatment effect is similar across remaining binary biomarkers (feature_002, feature_011, feature_022, feature_021, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027), indicating no further gating modifiers.

analyses.code: stratify responder subgroup by smoking, histology, feature_014, feature_023; compute treatment effect
analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup, treatment effect (mean1-mean0) is consistently large: smoking current +4.519, former +4.775, never +3.979 (NS heterogeneity); histology adenocarcinoma +4.665, squamous +4.642; feature_014 0 -> +4.570, 1 -> +4.767, 2 -> +4.534; feature_023 0 -> +4.709, 1 -> +4.623; feature_011 0 -> +4.702, 1 -> +4.468. No meaningful heterogeneity within responders.

analyses.code: stratify responder by feature_002, feature_022, feature_021, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect within responders by other binaries: feature_022 (0 -> +4.665, 1 -> +4.356), feature_002 (0 -> +4.669, 1 -> +4.487), feature_012 (0 -> +4.826, 1 -> +4.456), feature_020 (0 -> +4.602, 1 -> +4.740), feature_021 (0 -> +4.631, 1 -> +4.660), feature_027 (0 -> +4.585, 1 -> +4.785). All within ~0.4 months of one another; no further effect modification beyond the four-variable definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 has a continuous interaction with feature_018: the treatment benefit grows with higher feature_015 (interaction coef > 0).
Continuous lab features (feature_017, feature_025, feature_032, etc.) do not modify the feature_018 treatment effect at any clinically meaningful magnitude.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +0.0035 per unit feature_015 (p=0.010). Statistically significant in the full unadjusted model but small in magnitude relative to the main interactions, and not robust to including the responder subgroup. Likely a spillover from feature_015's correlation with PFS scale.

analyses.code: for c in continuous_labs: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018 * c').fit()
analyses.result_summary: Continuous-feature interaction p-values: feature_025 0.091, feature_003 0.142, feature_019 0.310, feature_007 0.313, feature_017 0.335, feature_009 0.375, feature_013 0.502, feature_010 0.506, feature_008 0.561, feature_032 0.565, feature_029 0.616, feature_024 0.627, feature_026 0.796, feature_033 0.844, feature_004 0.886. None significant. The treatment-effect heterogeneity is driven by the four binary modifiers identified, not by continuous features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 is concentrated in never smokers (feature_001 = 'never'); ~50% of never smokers carry it vs ~3% of current smokers.
feature_028 is also enriched in never smokers (~10%) compared with current/former smokers (~3-5%).
feature_016 is enriched in current smokers (~20%) compared with never smokers (~2%), showing the opposite pattern from feature_022 and feature_028.

analyses.result_summary: feature_022=1 prevalence: never 51.3%, former 8.1%, current 3.0% (chi-square p<1e-300). Strong inverse association with smoking exposure.
feature_028=1 prevalence: never 10.3%, former 4.8%, current 2.6% (chi-square p<1e-300). Same direction as feature_022 but lower prevalence.
feature_016=1 prevalence: current 19.7%, former 11.9%, never 2.0% (chi-square p<1e-300). Strong positive association with smoking exposure.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 has marginal balance across major prognostic covariates after stratification by histology and feature_023, supporting the interpretation as a randomized-like assignment within those clinical strata.
Other 'random-looking' binary features (feature_012, feature_020, feature_027) have no detectable treatment effect even after stratification, confirming they are not active treatments.

analyses.code: test feature_018 baseline balance within histology and feature_023 strata
analyses.result_summary: Within adenocarcinoma: feature_018=1 prevalence 34.96% vs 35.16% within squamous; within feature_023=0 35.05% vs feature_023=1 35.04%; within feature_014 levels 0/1/2: 35.4% / 35.0% / 33.8%. No meaningful imbalances; feature_018 is approximately as-randomized at every stratum.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_012 * feature_016', etc.) for each candidate
analyses.result_summary: Interaction tests of feature_012/020/027 with feature_016 and feature_031: all interaction p-values > 0.1. None of these binary indicators acts as a treatment in any subgroup. They appear to be either pure noise covariates or unmeasured arms with no PFS effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a fully adjusted multivariable model of pfs_months, the strongest independent predictors are feature_015 (positive), feature_023 (negative), feature_014 levels (negative), feature_011 (negative), feature_006 squamous (negative), feature_016 (positive), feature_001 smoking (negative for current), feature_025 (positive), feature_017 (negative), feature_018 (positive treatment), feature_031 (negative), and feature_032 (negative).
feature_002, feature_005, feature_019, feature_021, feature_022, feature_028 have minimal independent main effects on pfs_months after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Significant adjusted coefficients (months): feature_015 +0.168 per unit (p<1e-300), feature_023 -1.417 (p<1e-300), feature_014[T.1] -1.147, feature_014[T.2] -2.177, feature_011 -0.912, feature_006[T.squamous] -0.723, feature_016 +0.892, feature_001[T.former] +0.552, feature_001[T.never] +0.545, feature_025 +0.463, feature_018 +0.323, feature_031 -0.203, feature_032 -0.0004 per unit, feature_017 -0.073 per unit, feature_005 -0.109 (rare), feature_002 -0.019 (small).

analyses.code: extract p-values from full multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted p-values: feature_022 p=0.94, feature_019 p=0.67, feature_021 p=0.46, feature_028 p=0.061 (small negative), feature_002 p=0.037 (very small negative). After accounting for confounders, these features' main effects are negligible. feature_028's main effect is dominated by its role as treatment-effect modifier rather than as an independent prognostic factor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 is the only binary feature in the cohort that produces a clinically meaningful treatment-like effect on pfs_months in any subgroup; the other binary candidates (feature_012, feature_020, feature_027, feature_002) have null effects across all subgroups defined by the four key gating variables.

analyses.code: for cand in [feature_012, feature_020, feature_027, feature_002]: stratify by responder subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within the (feature_016=1, feature_031=0, feature_028=0, feature_005=0) stratum, treatment effects of other candidates: feature_012 (0 vs 1) +0.038 NS, feature_020 +0.014 NS, feature_027 +0.122 NS, feature_002 +0.085 NS. Outside the responder stratum, all are null. Only feature_018 produces the +5-month responder effect; no other binary feature behaves like an active treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among feature_016=1 patients, feature_031=1 indicates a state in which the feature_018 treatment cannot work, regardless of any other covariate; this is consistent with feature_031 being a tumor-intrinsic resistance-conferring marker.
Among feature_016=0 patients, feature_018 has no effect even when feature_031=0, indicating feature_016 is necessary (not sufficient) for response.

analyses.code: stratify feature_016=1 by feature_031 and feature_001 (smoking)
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1 AND feature_031=1, treatment effect of feature_018: former smokers diff +0.017 NS, current smokers diff -0.000 NS. Effect uniformly null. Adding any further covariate stratification cannot rescue treatment benefit when feature_031=1.

analyses.code: stratify feature_016=0 by feature_031, smoking, feature_028
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=0, feature_031=0: treatment effect = -0.019 NS; stratifying by feature_028, smoking, feature_023, feature_014 yields no subgroup with significant treatment benefit. feature_016=1 is necessary for any feature_018 response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 within the responder subgroup produces a multiplicative-like benefit: the median PFS roughly doubles or more (3.25 -> 8.23 months).
Outside the responder subgroup, feature_018's treatment effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero, with a 95% confidence interval excluding clinically meaningful benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup: mean PFS off treatment 3.249, on treatment 8.234, ratio = 2.53. Outside responder subgroup: ratio = 0.998. The subgroup defines a binary gate for treatment activity.

Non-responders (n=46734): feature_018=0 mean 3.323 (n=30344); feature_018=1 mean 3.317 (n=16390); diff = -0.006 (95% CI approximately -0.04 to +0.03). Effect tightly bounded around zero; clinically null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The univariate-significant lab feature_017 (negative) and feature_025 (positive) retain independent effects in the multivariable model, suggesting they capture true prognostic information beyond what is encoded in the demographic and disease-burden variables.
feature_032 has a statistically significant but tiny negative effect on PFS (-0.0004 months per unit) in the multivariable model.

analyses.code: examine adjusted coefficients on feature_017 and feature_025
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable-adjusted coefficients: feature_017 = -0.0732 per unit (p<1e-300), feature_025 = +0.463 per unit (p<1e-300). Both retain large independent effect sizes, with feature_025's per-unit coefficient implying ~1 month PFS difference across the IQR of feature_025 (3.5-4.1).

analyses.code: extract feature_032 coef from multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: feature_032 multivariable coefficient = -0.000446 per unit (p=2e-25). Across the IQR of feature_032 (~167-266), this corresponds to ~0.04-month PFS difference, statistically real but clinically negligible.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 has a positive coefficient of approximately +0.168 months per unit on pfs_months, accounting for the largest fraction of explained variance.

feature_023, feature_014, feature_011, and feature_006 squamous each contribute approximately 0.7-2.2 months of negative independent effect on PFS, identifying them as the dominant disease-burden / disease-aggressiveness predictors.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_015 quartile, compute mean PFS
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_015 quartile: Q1 1.79, Q2 2.62, Q3 3.79, Q4 5.49 (one-way ANOVA p<1e-300). Per-unit effect of +0.168 in linear model is consistent with strong, monotone positive prognostic effect.

analyses.code: extract feature_023, feature_014, feature_011, feature_006 coefs
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients (months): feature_023 -1.417, feature_014[1] -1.147, feature_014[2] -2.177, feature_011 -0.912, feature_006[squamous] -0.723. All p<1e-300. Together these encode the principal disease-burden axis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup, the absolute PFS gain on feature_018 is similar across baseline disease-burden levels (feature_023, feature_014, feature_011), indicating relative effect homogeneity.
Continuous lab values (feature_017, feature_025, feature_032) do not modify the feature_018 treatment effect within the responder subgroup.

analyses.code: stratify responders by feature_023 and feature_014, compute treatment effect
analyses.result_summary: Within responders, feature_018 treatment effect by feature_023 (0 vs 1): +4.709 vs +4.623; by feature_014 (0,1,2): +4.570, +4.767, +4.534; by feature_011 (0 vs 1): +4.702 vs +4.468. Effects within ~0.3 months of one another. Treatment benefit is largely homogeneous across disease-burden strata in responders.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018 * c', data=responders).fit() for c in continuous_labs
analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup, feature_018 x continuous-lab interaction p-values are all > 0.10 (no significant continuous modifiers). Treatment benefit is uniform across lab profiles within responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final integrated treatment-effect heterogeneity statement: feature_018 produces a clinically large benefit on pfs_months (+~5 months) only in patients who simultaneously have feature_016=1, feature_031=0, feature_028=0, and feature_005=0; it has no effect in any other patient subgroup.

The absence of feature_005 (i.e., feature_005=0) and the absence of feature_028 (feature_028=0) act as 'permissive' conditions; their presence (=1) acts as a treatment-effect veto similar in magnitude to absence of the primary marker feature_016.

analyses.result_summary: Final interaction model: feature_018 main effect = -0.006 (p=0.76), responder main effect = -0.075 (p=0.12), feature_018 x responder = +4.992 (p=0; t=62.5). The interaction is the dominant signal in the entire dataset for treatment effect.

analyses.code: compute treatment effect for f16=1, f31=0, f28=1 and f16=1, f31=0, f5=1
analyses.result_summary: Veto effects within feature_016=1, feature_031=0: feature_028=1 -> treatment diff = -0.285 (n=138, p=0.51); feature_005=1 (within f28=0) -> treatment diff = +0.053 (n=104, p=0.91). Both eliminate the response in their own ~3-5% subgroups, although small-sample confidence intervals are wide. Each is consistent with an independent veto of treatment activity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The gating subgroup definition (feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0) cannot be relaxed without diluting the treatment effect: relaxing any single predicate produces a qualitatively smaller benefit because of subgroup contamination by veto-positive patients.

All other binary indicators (feature_011, feature_022, feature_023, feature_002, feature_012, feature_020, feature_021, feature_027) and continuous lab values are NOT part of the responder definition: adding any of them as additional predicates does not increase the within-subgroup treatment effect.

analyses.code: for each predicate, drop and recompute treatment effect on relaxed subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Effect of relaxing one predicate at a time (treatment diff in resulting subgroup): drop f16=1 -> +0.048 (full cohort minus contamination); drop f31=0 -> +2.557 (responders + non-responders with f31=1); drop f28=0 -> +4.832 (small dilution); drop f5=0 -> +4.832 (small dilution). Both f16 and f31 are essential; f28 and f5 contribute only at the margin (~3-5% prevalence each).

analyses.code: for each remaining binary indicator, add as predicate and check effect size
analyses.result_summary: Adding feature_022=0 as a 5th predicate: within-subgroup effect remains +5.00 (vs +4.99 baseline). Adding feature_011=0: +5.05 (small chance gain). Adding feature_002=0: +4.99. Adding any continuous-feature stratum: no gain. The four-predicate definition is necessary and sufficient to identify the responsive subgroup; further refinement gives no meaningful gain in average treatment effect within the subgroup.
